voice_rules.title: First-line discipline
voice_rules.body: First 5 words decide the watch. Either a specific number, a vulnerable admission, a pointed take, or a named anomaly. No setup, no "Hi, today I want to talk about..."

voice_rules.title: One sentence per beat
voice_rules.body: TikTok pace doesn't carry multi-clause sentences the way prose does. Break it.

voice_rules.title: Cut "I think" / "for me" / "personally."
voice_rules.body: They soften the take. The take is yours by virtue of you saying it. Don't double-flag.

voice_rules.title: Specifics over scaffolding
voice_rules.body: "5:25am" beats "early morning." "One kid, second on the way" beats "growing family." "Three sessions with Claude" beats "with AI."

voice_rules.title: The close is a plain statement, not a CTA
voice_rules.body: "Posting the work here so I keep going" beats "Follow for more!"

voice_rules.title: No music carrying the message
voice_rules.body: Voice carries. Music is a bed.

voice_rules.title: Captions match what you say
voice_rules.body: Don't paraphrase yourself on-screen. Either the caption is the same words, or don't caption that line.

pillars.label: Design / craft
pillars.scope: Type pairing, brand tokens, Figma, design judgement, governance over generation, UI taste calls
pillars.example_subjects: Sweat redesign, Money.com.au, Grays.com.au, a typeface decision, a brand system trade-off

pillars.label: Dad
pillars.scope: Parenting layer, family rhythm, structured freedom in practice
pillars.example_subjects: Rowie (born Oct 2024), Hannah, second kid due September 2026, 5:25am, bath time, the engineered day

pillars.label: Founder
pillars.scope: Building things you own, shipping moments, decisions and trade-offs
pillars.example_subjects: Lia, NOBONK packaging, Voxquote, OpenScripture, robson.studio, Loupe, freelance

pillars.label: Tastemaker
pillars.scope: What good looks like and why. Trains the viewer's eye
pillars.example_subjects: Vignelli, Eames, Eno, 1970s skateboarding, restraint, a building in Newcastle, a typeface, a campaign in the wild

pillars.label: AI / tooling
pillars.scope: How tools actually work in your hand
pillars.example_subjects: Claude (CLAUDE.md, skills folder), Cursor, Tokens Studio, Figma + AI workflows, agentic patterns, Wispr Flow

topics_in_by_pillar.design-craft: Typography, brand systems, UI judgement, governance over generation, design taste calls, real client work anonymised specifically ("a fintech design system rollout"), Figma craft, the senior-designer-with-AI thesis.
topics_in_by_pillar.dad: The 5:25am routine, structured freedom in practice (the day engineered backwards from bedtime), parenting choices (authoritative approach, high warmth high standards), the second pregnancy, what it actually takes to keep deep work and family running together.
topics_in_by_pillar.founder: Building Lia (in-progress, specific decisions, what's hard), NOBONK packaging and brand work (project work, not personal product, per 5 May decision), Voxquote scope generation pipeline, OpenScripture MVP, robson.studio launch, freelance moments, decisions made and trade-offs accepted.
topics_in_by_pillar.tastemaker: A designer Dan respects (and why specifically), a building, a typeface, a campaign in the wild, photography references, Newcastle as place (the light, the pace, specific spots), restraint as a design value, references from outside design (skateboarding, music, architecture).
topics_in_by_pillar.ai-tooling: How tools actually work in your hand: Claude with CLAUDE.md + skills folder, Cursor for coding, Tokens Studio in the design-system loop, Wispr Flow for dictation, Figma + AI workflows, agentic patterns, the prompts and setups that are actually doing work (not abstract takes).

topics_out: Religion / faith deconstruction. Held back until the page has trust (video 8-12 earliest per [[02-ideas-log]])
NOBONK as a personal-brand chapter (it's freelance, not the next chapter)
Compassion field photography. Held back until the page has trust
The Lia ICP-specific design-governance take in raw form (sharpen for Lia content separately)
Anything that needs more than 60s to land properly (that's YouTube material, not TikTok)
Hot takes for the sake of hot takes

hook_bank.template: Evidence shows (tiny behaviour) reduces (important outcome).
hook_bank.fit_notes: AI / tooling, Design / craft. Works only with real evidence.
hook_bank.example_label: sahilbloom — Your phone is destroying your relationships

hook_bank.template: You are not responsible for (other people's reaction to your growth).
There's a name for (the thing you're feeling).
The real (status trait) isn't (obvious thing). It's (something controllable).
hook_bank.fit_notes: Tastemaker, Founder. Careful, can read gnomic.
hook_bank.example_label: sahilbloom — This is the most attractive trait

hook_bank.template: Open with the workflow itself, no setup.
Beginner does X. Intermediate does Y. Senior does Z.
X wasn't meant to be Y, but that's what makes it iconic." Five-beat: anomaly, origin, name, turning point, cultural moment.
I have N days to do M." Numbers do the work.
Voice the take you disagree with, then counter from personal experience.
Caption tells the arc, visuals prove. No piece-to-camera monologue. Low-cringe credibility posts.
Open with a memorable external quote, then your read.
I'm genuinely terrible at being my own client.
I feel a bit silly talking to a camera. Here's why I'm doing it anyway.
Hardest thing I've tried: building products that earn while I'm at bedtime.
I'm 30. One kid, second on the way. And I just quit being someone else's employee in my head.
I'm not scared of failing. I'm scared of stagnating during the biggest tech shift of my lifetime.
I've used AI on every project this year. The job hasn't shrunk, it's shifted.

common_to_hook.common: I'm a UX designer at Endava working on Lia
common_to_hook.hook: Hardest design problem I've taken on: a product for musicians, while still designing for SaaS clients.

common_to_hook.common: AI is changing how I design
common_to_hook.hook: I've used AI on every project this year. Here's what actually changed about the job.

common_to_hook.common: I built a small clothing company
common_to_hook.hook: I sold t-shirts that scared away mosquitoes for two years. Here's what it taught me about brand.

common_to_hook.common: Working on Lia with CQ and Luke
common_to_hook.hook: Three friends, three days off, one question: should we actually do this?

common_to_hook.common: Structured freedom is my operating philosophy
common_to_hook.hook: I'm trying to build a life where what I earn isn't tied to the hours I work. Six months in, here's what's working.

common_to_hook.common: I'm scared about building a company with two kids
common_to_hook.hook: I'm scared of stagnating during the biggest tech shift of my lifetime. So I'm building.
AI generates 200 versions in seconds. Senior design now is choosing which one ships.

common_to_hook.common: Newcastle is a good place to live
common_to_hook.hook: Five reasons I work better in Newcastle than I did in Melbourne.
Up at 5:25. Gym by 5:45. Why I built the day backwards from bedtime.

common_to_hook.common: I'm running my own ideas like client work
common_to_hook.hook: I built my studio site in three sessions after sitting on the Figma for two years. Here's what changed.

signals: Title starts with `tt:` (the established prefix from [[00-workflow]]) or contains "tiktok"
Body mentions hook, video idea, script, post, reel, vlog, or a creator handle

routes.destination: Hook bank section above (`flag: audit-needed` on first pass)
routes.notes: Dan confirms or strikes during Sunday review
